prompt: Develop a PetMarket Operations & Risk Management system for an offline pet marketplace and service catalog where shoppers can discover listings and staff can control content, conversations, and settlement. The system supports three roles: Shoppers (browse and inquire), Vendors (manage listings and handle customer conversations), and Operations Admins (moderate content, manage campaigns, approve settlements, and run exports). The React web interface provides a fast search experience with keyword search, autocomplete suggestions, optional typo correction for common misspellings, and multi-dimensional filters and sorting such as breed, age, region, price range in USD, rating, and “new arrivals” within the last 14 days; when a query returns no results, the UI shows fallback recommendations based on similar breeds and trending rankings from the last 7 days. Vendors and Shoppers collaborate through an in-app conversation workspace that supports text and recorded voice notes (uploaded as local audio files), full context history per listing, canned responses configured by Operations, shared internal notes visible only to Vendors and Admins, and archiving with conversation search by keyword and date range. Operations Admins use a configuration console to publish announcements, schedule campaigns, manage homepage carousel and recommendation slots with start/end times, and run sensitive-word filtering that blocks publishing when prohibited terms appear, prompting a review-required state. Power users can run data queries with combined conditions, saved custom lists, sortable columns, and paginated results with clear loading states; exports run asynchronously with an on-screen job queue showing progress, and downloaded files include permission-based watermarking and masking (for example, only the last 4 digits of phone numbers and partial street addresses for non-admin roles). The backend uses NestJS to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend, enforcing role-based access and input validation while persisting data in PostgreSQL for fully offline operation. A credit and risk control framework computes a local credit score per Vendor and Shopper using trailing 90-day metrics such as transaction success rate, dispute rate, and cancellation rate, and applies configurable policies including rate limits (for example, max 30 listing posts per hour and max 10 new conversations per 10 minutes per account) plus abnormal behavior rules like frequent reposting of near-duplicate listings, multi-account signals, device fingerprint reuse, and IP risk flags (stored locally; no external lookups). The charges and settlement engine calculates freight estimates and internal cost allocation using tiered rules combining distance in miles, billable weight in pounds, and dimensional weight, with surcharges such as oversized (+$15.00) and weekend handling (+5%); it generates monthly statements on the first day of each month, supports variance reconciliation against expected vs. actual charges, routes settlements through a two-step approval (Ops Reviewer then Finance Admin), includes sales tax fields where applicable, and provides export endpoints for accounting downloads without integrating online payments. Data query exports are executed as background jobs stored in PostgreSQL with concurrency capped (for example, 2 exports at a time) and automatic expiration of export files after 7 days. End-to-end audit and compliance traceability records create/update/delete/import/approve actions with timestamp, actor, device fingerprint, and before/after snapshots; logs are append-only with hash chaining for tamper-evident verification and retained for 7 years, searchable in an admin audit console with export controls. All authentication is local username/password only, passwords are salted and hashed, sensitive fields are encrypted at rest, and masked by default in responses unless the requester has explicit permission.
